2016-04-25.abstract: A pristine valley in Yunnan Province, home to the ethnic Dulong people, is seeing progress in the name of alleviating poverty and drawing tourists.

2016-04-25.title: Austrian Presidential Vote Gives Edge to Right-Wing Candidate
2016-04-25.abstract: Norbert Hofer of the anti-immigration Freedom Party was the leader in the first round of voting, winning 35 percent of the ballots. A runoff is set for May 22.

2016-04-25.title: Missing Mexican Students Suffered a Night of ‘Terror,’ Investigators Say
2016-04-25.abstract: An international panel has provided details of the night in 2014 when Mexican police and gunmen abducted 43 students who have not been seen since.

2016-04-25.title: Inquiry Challenges Mexico’s Account of How 43 Students Vanished
2016-04-25.abstract: Five suspects whose testimony underpinned the government’s conclusions gave confessions “under torture or cruel, inhumane or degrading treatment,” a report by international experts found.

2016-04-26.title: Qi Benyu, Mao Aide Unrepentant About Cultural Revolution, Dies at 84
2016-04-26.abstract: Mr. Qi, a propagandist in the Chinese Communist Party, expressed no remorse about the brutalities under Mao Zedong and remained a steadfast admirer of him.

2016-04-26.title: Iran Threatens Lawsuit in Hague Court Over U.S. Ruling on $2 Billion
2016-04-26.abstract: It is unclear, however, whether the International Court of Justice will have jurisdiction in Iran’s complaint.

2016-04-26.title: Afghanistan’s Vice President Is Barred From Entering U.S.
2016-04-26.abstract: Officials threatened to deny a visa to Abdul Rashid Dostum, who is accused of war crimes but is also the No. 2 official in a government whose survival depends on American troops and money.

2016-04-26.title: Few Expect Mexico’s Government to Suffer at Polls, Despite Outrage Over Abductions
2016-04-26.abstract: As protests have dwindled over the official investigation into the disappearance of 43 students, many fear the ruling party will face few political consequences.

2016-04-26.title: ISIS Spreading in Europe, U.S. Intelligence Chief Warns
2016-04-26.abstract: James R. Clapper Jr. warned of clandestine cells in Britain, Germany and Italy resembling the groups that carried out the Paris and Brussels terrorist attacks.

2016-04-27.title: Colombia Confirms More Birth Defects Linked to Zika
2016-04-27.abstract: Both mothers had the Zika virus during pregnancy, but Colombian health officials said overall cases of the mosquito-borne virus were decreasing there.

2016-04-27.title: Venezuela Panel Clears the Way for a Process to Oust Nicolás Maduro
2016-04-27.abstract: A decision by the country’s electoral commission lifted hopes of the opposition politicians, who have vowed to remove the president by the end of the year.

2016-04-27.title: Israel’s Vow to Keep Golan Brings ‘Deep Concern’ at Security Council
2016-04-27.abstract: The Security Council president said Resolution 497, which described Israel’s annexation of the Golan Heights as ‘null and void,’ remains in effect.

2016-04-27.title: 5 Rescue Workers Are Killed in Northern Syria
2016-04-27.abstract: Five rescue workers with a civil defense group were killed in aerial attacks on their headquarters in northern Syria on Tuesday as violence escalated in Aleppo Province.

2016-04-27.title: North Korea to Convene Rare Party Congress Next Week
2016-04-27.abstract: The congress, the first since 1980, is a likely platform for the North’s leader, Kim Jong-un, to cement his authority.

2016-04-28.title: Flooded With Migrants, Germany Struggles to Integrate Them
2016-04-28.abstract: More than a million migrants arrived in Germany. Now, the task is to decide who stays and who goes.

2016-04-28.title: Angela Merkel Abruptly Fires Germany’s Spy Chief
2016-04-28.abstract: The dismissal of Gerhard Schindler came when his job appeared safe, despite his agency’s links to carrying out searches at the behest of the United States’ National Security Agency.

2016-04-28.title: U.N. Envoy Prods U.S. and Russia to ‘Revitalize’ Syria Cease-Fire
2016-04-28.abstract: The truce, now crumbling, sharply reduced hostilities in March and allowed aid groups to deliver humanitarian supplies to starving civilians.

2016-04-28.title: Q. and A.: Tomas Plänkers on the Psychic Legacy of the Cultural Revolution
2016-04-28.abstract: Unlike postwar Germany or post-Communist Eastern Europe, China cannot openly face the baggage of its past, the Cultural Revolution, a psychoanalyst says.

2016-04-28.title: ‘Doctor Strange’ Writer Says China-Tibet Remarks Don’t Represent Marvel
2016-04-28.abstract: C. Robert Cargill said his statement that a wish to avoid offending China contributed to removing Tibetan aspects of the Ancient One was only his personal musing.

2016-04-29.title: Iran Hints at Retaliation Over U.S. Use of Seized Assets
2016-04-29.abstract: A letter to the United Nations secretary general by Iran’s foreign minister was among the most strident yet in a steadily increasing display of anger.

2016-04-29.title: Obama-Netanyahu Rift Impedes U.S. Offer of Record Aid Deal for Israel
2016-04-29.abstract: President Obama has proposed the largest package of military aid ever provided by the United States, but Israel has insisted on more generous terms.

2016-04-29.title: North Korea Sentences American to 10 Years for Spying, Reports Say
2016-04-29.abstract: Kim Dong-chul is the latest United States citizen to receive a harsh sentence in North Korea, which often uses the fates of held Americans as leverage.

2016-04-29.title: Refugee Held on Nauru Dies After Self-Immolation to Protest Australian Policy
2016-04-29.abstract: The man, a 23-year-old Iranian kept from Australia, was airlifted to a hospital there, where he died from his injuries, the immigration department said.

2016-04-29.title: Debt Collectors Adopt Mob Tactics as Russians Struggle to Pay Bills
2016-04-29.abstract: A culture of violence and threats flourishes as growing numbers of Russians turn to borrowing at astronomical interest rates amid a recession.

2016-04-30.title: Ending Impasse, Irish Leaders Agree on a Government
2016-04-30.abstract: The agreement, which follows an inconclusive election and two months of political deadlock, is likely to result in the re-election of Enda Kenny as prime minister.

2016-04-30.title: U.S. Rejects Iran’s Protest on Asset Ruling
2016-04-30.abstract: The State Department said a Supreme Court ruling allowing use of Iranian assets to compensate victims of terrorist attacks comports with international law.

2016-04-30.title: U.N. Renews Mission in Western Sahara in Divisive Vote
2016-04-30.abstract: A Security Council resolution renewed the peacekeeping mission but exerted little pressure on Morocco, which expelled dozens of United Nations workers last month.

2016-04-30.title: Protesters Storm Baghdad’s Green Zone to Denounce Corruption
2016-04-30.abstract: Hundreds breached the razor wire of Baghdad’s fortified Green Zone, long seen as a symbol of a government detached from ordinary Iraqi citizens.

2016-04-30.title: Muslim’s Labour Party Candidacy Shapes London Mayoral Race
2016-04-30.abstract: With Britain struggling to integrate minorities, Sadiq Khan is favored to win election on Thursday, which would make him the first Muslim to lead the city and give a boost to his opposition party.
